input.text: P&G is a portfolio of everyday brands that earn trust through repeated daily use.

company.summary: The company brief is grounded in daily-use categories, brand trust, and broad household relevance.

input.text: Write with measured optimism, concrete examples, and operator-grade specificity.

voice.summary: The voice should sound analytical, warm, and practical rather than hype-driven.

input.text: Avoid unsourced medical claims, avoid absolute statements, and cite source-derived claims with footnotes.

guardrails.summary: The guardrails require careful sourcing, restrained claims, and explicit footnotes where evidence is used.

researchSources.title: Household rituals are replacing productivity hacks
researchSources.author: Dana Fields
researchSources.snippet: Consumer researchers are seeing mundane tasks reframed as rituals that reduce stress and create a feeling of control.

researchSources.title: Value is being redefined as reliability plus delight
researchSources.author: Mina Ortega

outline.title: Routines are turning into brand moats
outline.dek: What trusted consumer brands can learn from ritual-shaped demand.

sections.heading: Rituals signal where relevance is moving
bullets: Consumers want less friction and more reassurance in everyday tasks.
Rituals reveal why repeat categories still matter.

sections.heading: Reliable delight beats generic premium
bullets: Reliability is now a premium cue when budgets are tight.
Small moments of delight can protect margin.

sections.heading: Systems thinking changes the post format
bullets: Talk about routines and systems, not isolated SKUs.

writeCycles.content:
# Routines are turning into brand moats

Consumers are rewarding products that make repetitive tasks feel calmer, simpler, and more competent.[1]

## Reliable delight is the new premium

When shoppers describe value, they increasingly blend price discipline with dependable performance and small sensory rewards.[2]

## Systems beat single-product stories

Winning brands show how products fit into a repeatable care system rather than a one-off purchase.[3]

# Routines are turning into brand moats

Consumers are rewarding products that make repetitive tasks feel calmer, simpler, and more competent.[1]

## Reliable delight is the new premium

Value no longer means cheapness alone. It means dependable outcomes plus a small emotional payoff that justifies sticking with the brand.[2]

## Systems beat single-product stories

Winning brands show how products fit into a repeatable care system rather than a one-off purchase.[3]

# Routines are turning into brand moats

Consumers are rewarding products that make repetitive tasks feel calmer, simpler, and more competent.[1]

## Reliable delight is the new premium

Value no longer means cheapness alone. It means dependable outcomes plus a small emotional payoff that can reinforce loyalty when the product consistently performs.[2]

## Systems beat single-product stories

Winning brands show how products fit into a repeatable care system rather than a one-off purchase.[3]

finalPost.subtitle: Why trusted brands should write about systems, not just SKUs.
